title: Liar's Poker
subtitle: Rising Through the Wreckage on Wall Street
author: Michael Lewis
authorBio: Michael Lewis (b. 1960) is an American author and financial journalist. A Princeton and LSE graduate, he worked at Salomon Brothers in the 1980s before becoming a full-time writer. His books have sold millions of copies worldwide and include The Big Short, Moneyball, Flash Boys, and The Blind Side. Several have been adapted into Oscar-winning films.
notableQuotes: The people who could get you a loan were not the people you wanted to borrow from.
Wall Street was not a place where you could park a moral sensibility.
The things you are taught are the things that will soon be obsolete.
There is only one sin on Wall Street: losing money.
Liar's Poker was a microcosm of the bond market itself.
